author: Mark Frost
text: 2026-07-18 ROOT CAUSE CONFIRMED: the T1000-E misses the gateway's response to its OWN GET (half-duplex TX->RX turnaround), while overhearing responses to other nodes. Proof chain: (1) Gateway fully works - instrumented status_get shows 'status GET from ..2c:ab -> respond 231 B ret=0' for the T1000-E, each immediately followed by 'lichen_l2: TX -> ..2c:ab (193 B)', so the gateway receives the T1000-E's GET, the CoAP server responds, and L2 transmits the response to the T1000-E over the air. (2) Isolation test: with the gateway pinning ONLY the T1000-E (so its ONLY TXs are responses to the T1000-E), the T1000-E's l2rx.verified FROZE at 106 = it received ZERO of those responses. (3) In 2-peer, verified climbs only because the T1000-E overhears gateway->T-Echo responses (which arrive while it is idle-listening between its own GET cycles), never its own (which arrive right after it TXes its GET, during turnaround). So: gateway TXes the response (proven) + T1000-E receives none of its own (proven) => the T1000-E is not in RX when its response arrives. The gateway responds within <1ms of the GET (same-ms log), and the SF10 response follows immediately after the T1000-E's GET airtime ends; the LR1110 RX thread does not re-arm lora_recv() fast enough after the GET TX to catch it. FIX OPTIONS: (a) add an RX1-style response delay on the gateway (wait ~50-100ms after RX before TXing the reply, giving the requester time to switch to RX) - matches LoRaWAN class-A timing; (b) speed up the T1000-E LR1110 TX->RX re-arm (the recv() re-arms set_rx continuous, but the modem-mutex handoff from the TX + the 20ms poll granularity may add latency); (c) CoAP client retransmit will eventually align by phase drift but clearly is not, so timing is systematic not random. NOTE: the T-Echo (SX1262) does NOT have this problem (completes round trips), so the LR1110 turnaround/re-arm is slower than the SX1262's, OR the LR1110 recv re-arm path is the bottleneck. Diagnostics committed: gateway status_get GET/respond log + lichen_l2 unicast-LL TX-dest log + the lr1110/l2rx/coap STATS groups.
